item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to agree with some of the previous posts on this being an interesting idea but lacking detail and background. The idea of an incarnate Pestilence walking the Earth should really have more meat on the bones (I'm so very sorry for that one). No vote for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty creepy and curious idea. I do wish there was a bit more involving further attempts to communicate, and what the thing does in its free time, but I'll upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
interesting. this guy is in deed the body of pestilence, but he seems to lack the power. Also, if you put the bug man in a potato sack and give him a gambling addition, I'll track you down, find you, and make you watch "The Nightmare Before Christmas" over and over again until your DNA remembers it, ruining the movie for all your future offspring. then I would down vote. I'm warning you, whoever wrote this article.

as you can tell, I read the chats from before.

also, how does it hold a pen or pencil? what ever, I don't dislike anything about it so far, but I don't find it worth an up vote, so no vote for now, but I'll be back. maybe. I don't know. let's just agree I'm insane and call it a day. or night.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I felt it ended too quickly as well. What sort of things does he write? Is he happy being composed of bugs, was he once human?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is okay. The idea is certainly interesting, but it feels incomplete for me. Maybe it needs an interview log, or an attempted breach of containment. I'm a no-vote on this one for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why did the BSL label make me think of Metroid Fusion immediately?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why do I always get one of these right before lunch? Upvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's true.

He won't have lobsters, crabs, jellyfish, those deep-sea red wormy things with the bacteria or shrimp/prawns, although he will, oddly enough, have velvet worms and woodlice. He won't have sea slugs, thoguh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
composed entirely of invertebrate organisms from many species.

A nitpick: this should be changed to say "terrestrial invertebrate", so it's clear the poor guy doesn't have any jellyfish or shrimp in him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oogie Boogie.

I'm sorry, but this is the first thing I thought of, and no one else mentioned it. It had to be said.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: oh, his pun, not mine. Okay.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, no, it was intentional. I opted not to do the glasses thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He said that it was bugging him…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's so unintentional I don't even see it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, what a pun! I will choose to assume it was unintentional. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The difference is, 906 is a bunch of bugs that occasionally take the shape of a man. This is a man made out of bugs.

A subtle but real distinction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Implemented the edit you suggested. Thank you :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No tags?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: YES! Thank you. That was bugging the hell out of me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That would be SCP-906.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First point of order, I could swear we already had something just like this. It was a gestalt of thousands upon thousands of worms that could slither into a humanoid form, with a (pretty decent) picture of said humanoid form. It was predatory, and hunted by corralling its prey and following through vents, piping, and such, and secreted a highly acidic substance to dissolve what it caught. When it was first posted, there was an entirely unnecessary testing log about how hard it was to kill. It also had an addendum wherein the thing had said a researcher's name.

I could just about swear it was rated in the teens the last time I saw it, but I can't recall its name or number, and the site only allows you to view all pages with one tag, not pages tagged with multiple things. If anyone else remembers this and can throw a link up, or clarify why it's gone, that'd be fantastic.

Second point of order:

Variola major, Mycobacterium leprae, Yersinia pestis, and Zaire ebolavirus

This is, quite simply, a walking level 4 biohazard. You say:

SCP-1249's containment cell is to be cleaned and disinfected at least twice daily by personnel wearing level-5 hazmat suits.

Standards exist for this sort of thing already. They may be found here.

Type 5/Level D protection does not protect the crew member from chemical exposure. Therefore, this level of protection can only be used in situations where a crew member has no possibility of contact with chemicals. A pair of coveralls or other work-type garment along with chemical-resistant footwear with steel toes and shanks are all that is required to qualify as Level D protection. Most firefighter turnout gear is considered to be Level D.

That's apparently what you've got dudes wearing while dealing with a walking level 4. They should be wearing Type 1/Level A hazmat gear.

This thing in general should be treated as a level 4 biohazard. The procedures for dealing with such are rigorously defined already. This should give you a bit of an idea. You don't need to go into exhaustive detail about how level 4 biohazards are treated in the containment procedures, but just mentioning it is a class 4 biohazard, it's kept in a BSL-4 sector, and revising some of the procedures to be consistent with such would be appropriate, I think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
bloated bloodsucking leeches

Wait, what are they sucking the blood of?

I'm sorry, it just that this… bugged me.

*braces self against incoming rotten vegetables*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Pestilence"? I was betting on "The Worm That Walks". :P

I kinda like this but… gonna wait before voting anything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the discussion page for SCP-1249. Not for manga translations. -yoric

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the discussion page for SCP-1249. Not for manga translations. -yoric

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the discussion page for SCP-1249. Not for manga translations. -yoric

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the discussion page for SCP-1249. Not for manga translations. -yoric

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I misspoke. I should've said that both those tropes — the Wandering Jew and the Worm That Walks — are far older than manga. Older than D&D, too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought so too, aside from the "it has a human skeleton" bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wrote the book on the bee meme, and it didn't even occur to me. That being said, this doesn't particularly interest me, especially considering the Vermin God is this but with a feeling of humanity to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, is this a reference to something? I like the body horror sort of thing, as well as the image of a human-shaped pile of bugs scattering when you turn on the lights, but I don't recognize what it's referencing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey, hey hey HEY! I object! Centipedes and scorpions eat other, significantly more pestilent bugs, and usually only directly harm humans through extreme negligence or bad luck.

…. Other than that though, *shudder*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Your list just me me feel slightly ill. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: well… original wandering jew wasn't worm-filled, though

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That trope is far, far older than manga.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Isn't this the Wandering Jew from Franken Fran?

In the Manga Franken Fran the Wandering Jew was immortal and walked the earth so long that his organs were replaced with vermin. I'm not sure if you drew inspiration from this, but it's certainly an interesting take on the concept.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reminds me of The Worm that Walks from DnD 3.5 Elder Evils supplement and elsewhere.
That said, not bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For the love of all that is holy, *nobody* let 073 or 076 know about this one…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Might find this more interesting if there were various cephalopods, crustaceans, etc. thrown into the mix, instead of just terrestrial invertebrates. The disease thing seems tacked on for added dangerousness. I know he's supposed to be Pestilence incarnate, but there are at least two or three other SCPs that already fit that description.

Without the religious allusion, I can't help reading this as "Mr. Bugs, by Dr. Wondertainment"… which might actually be a better approach.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, it isn't, and it shouldn't. Memes are not a good basis for a serious article. Besides, the item can use any invertebrate for its body, not just bees.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
He's not made of bees; the ancient Israelites knew that bees were useful and made honey. In fact, I'd venture a guess that he's unable to assimilate bees - or butterflies, or ladybugs. Anything traditionally perceived as useful or attractive.

He's made of vermin. Houseflies and horseflies and bluebottles and cockroaches and centipedes and scorpions and fleas and ticks and lice and mites and tapeworms and silverfish and termites and mosquitoes and leeches and slugs and gnats and weevils, all squirming and writhing over each other and biting and stinging and parasitizing and shitting and festering and oozing slime, every second of every minute of every hour of every day of every year, for hundreds if not thousands of years.

Imagine what it would be like to know that even your brain is made of shit-eating roaches and slime-oozing slugs and bloated bloodsucking leeches and writhing poisonous centipedes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good god, this one made me shudder. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This isn't awful, but 'person made of bugs' has been done before, in myriad ways. Nothing sets this one apart.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a guy.

Made of BEES.

…downvoted. Old meme is old.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the concept somewhat, and the idea of a human skeleton with bugs as tissue creeps me out. The translation at the end, though, really needs more to it. I'm having a hard time finding the hook. Too vague as it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, is this not intentionally referencing the bee meme?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really want to take this one seriously, but the picture breaks it for me. Mostly because my brain automatically fills in:

We are cursed by Him
He [Unable to translate] vow
[Unable to translate]…ni Adam
Only then I was bees

I upvoted anyway, since it's written well and being bees is a cruddy reason for me to not upvote, but I felt I ought to let you know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kind of, but not really? I literally haven't played it in years, but I remember it being more like a single-player version of the MMO adventuring grind, with stick figure graphics and a text-based interface, but so tongue-in-cheekly humorous that it's not the sort of game you'd really be playing for the graphics or the interface, anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nah. I swear to god its not.

In fact… what is Kingdom of Loathing anyway? I've never heard about it until you gave me the link. Is it some sort of MMORPG?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks Esko, your help is really appreciated. I've just finished Implementing (read: copy and paste) your edit.

Thanks alot. Good luck on your SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I took one look and this is the first thing that comes to mind.

I REALLY hope this wasn't your inspiration.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm going to upvote, but if you're interested, I've copyedited this and posted it on my sandbox at http://sandbox.scp-wiki.net/eskobar on the very top. You should be able to either read it and make the changes (mostly subject/verb and numerical issues) or copy and paste it in here. But people seem to be upvoting regardless, so you don't seem to really need to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damn, I had something like this planned. Good job though. I wouldn't mind seeing the rest of the horsemen done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not confident with this one…. I'm just going to sit back and wait until the bomb goes off.

I will make the effort to improve this SCP based on feedback, though.
